title: China's Xi promotes China's commitment to AI access in speech at Shanghai conference
description: Chinese President Xi Jinping emphasised ​the importance of a "people-centred" ‌approach to the development of AI technology in ​a keynote address ​at the opening ceremony ⁠of the World Artificial ​Intelligence Conference (WAIC) in ​Shanghai on Friday.

title: Xi Jinping's Vision for People-Centric AI
description: Chinese President Xi Jinping highlighted the necessity of focusing on people in AI development during the World Artificial Intelligence Conference in Shanghai. He outlined China's commitment to equitable AI capacity building globally, emphasizing collaboration with international bodies to ensure fair access to AI opportunities, particularly for developing countries.
content: During the World Artificial Intelligence Conference in Shanghai, Chinese President Xi Jinping underscored the significance of a ‘people-centred’ approach to AI technology development. His keynote address marked an important moment for the future of A... [564 chars]

title: China’s Xi Jinping addresses World AI Conference as US tech rivalry heats up
description: President’s personal attendance seen as a sign of China’s inclusion of artificial intelligence into broader geopolitical strategy.
content:
President Xi Jinping will speak on Friday at the opening of the 2026 World Artificial Intelligence Conference (WAIC), China’s largest annual event for the AI industry.

title: Global Times: 300 plus AI products set to make their global debuts at WAIC with strong focus on practical applications
description: BEIJING, July 17, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- In Shanghai, the final countdown to one of the world's largest artificial intelligence (AI) events has entered its last stage. Inside the exhibition halls, interactive screens are being tested, robotic arms are undergoing repeated adjustments, and engineers are making final preparations before thousands of AI products meet global visitors, the Global Times observed while walking through the exhibition halls on Thursday.

title: Global Market Today: Asian stocks drop after chip selloff, oil gains
description: Asian stocks declined as chipmakers pulled down Wall Street amid valuation concerns. Oil prices climbed higher this week due to escalating Middle East hostilities. Investors are questioning the sustainability of massive artificial intelligence investments and tech valuations. Heightened geopolitical tensions fueled inflation worries and potential interest rate hikes. Consumers continue spending and the labor market shows resilience despite economic challenges.

title: Securing the foundations of AI
description: Artificial intelligence is exposing a weakness that has little to do with the technology itself.

title: C-DAC anchors India’s bid for Nvidia-like artificial intelligence chip
description: India’s first, indigenous AI inference chip will be made and presented as early as 2029 or 2030, Union IT minister Ashwini Vaishnaw said on Wednesday after the Cabinet approved a ₹1.27 trillion Semicon 2.0 mission to incentivize chip factories, startups, design firms and research.

title: MLB cracks down on dugout iPads with artificial intelligence fears growing
description: With AI fears growing, MLB is cracking down on the use of league-provided dugout iPads.
content:
MLB is cracking down on artificial intelligence.

title: New California law requires human teachers, not AI
description: California's new law ensures K-12 students are taught by human teachers, not artificial intelligence.
content:
California is drawing a line in the digital sand.
Starting 2027, public schools in the Golden State can’t have AI teachers for K-12 students.

title: Appeals Court Warns Bankruptcy Lawyer Over Alleged AI Citations
description: A federal appeals court admonished, but declined to sanction, an attorney for a brief containing what one judge called “an astonishing number” of citations that were incorrect or possibly hallucinated by artificial intelligence.
